response:
Here is a complete pytest test suite that covers all requested cases, with explicit naming and documentation of function behaviour:
```python
import pytest
# Replace with actual import from your source module
from your_source_file import remove_duplicates


class TestRemoveDuplicates:
    # ==============================================
    # 🟢 HAPPY PATH TESTS
    # ==============================================
    def test_removes_duplicates_preserves_insertion_order(self):
        """Core functionality: removes duplicates, keeps FIRST occurrence order.
        This is the key difference vs naive `list(set(input))` implementations."""
        input_list = [3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9, 3, 5]
        
        result = remove_duplicates(input_list)
        
        assert result == [3, 1, 4, 5, 9]
        # Verify original input is NEVER modified
        assert input_list == [3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9, 3, 5]

    def test_no_duplicates_returns_unchanged(self):
        """List with already unique items returns an identical copy"""
        test_data = ["apple", "banana", "cherry", 42]
        assert remove_duplicates(test_data) == test_data

    # ==============================================
    # 🟡 EDGE CASE TESTS
    # ==============================================
    def test_empty_input_list(self):
        """Empty list correctly returns empty list"""
        assert remove_duplicates([]) == []

    def test_single_element_input(self):
        """List with only one item works correctly"""
        assert remove_duplicates([9999]) == [9999]

    def test_all_elements_identical(self):
        """List where every entry is a duplicate"""
        assert remove_duplicates(["test", "test", "test", "test"]) == ["test"]

    def test_handles_none_values(self):
        """None is properly handled as a hashable value"""
        input_list = [None, 1, None, 3, None]
        assert remove_duplicates(input_list) == [None, 1, 3]

    def test_boolean_int_equality_behaviour(self):
        """Documents standard Python set behaviour: 1==True, 0==False"""
        # This is NOT a bug, this is core Python hash equality rules
        input_list = [1, True, 0, False]
        assert remove_duplicates(input_list) == [1, 0]

    def test_mixed_hashable_types(self):
        """Works correctly with valid mixed hashable types"""
        input_list = ["hello", 5, 3.14, (1, 2), "hello", 5, (1, 2)]
        assert remove_duplicates(input_list) == ["hello", 5, 3.14, (1, 2)]

    # ==============================================
    # 🔴 ERROR CASE TESTS
    # ==============================================
    def test_raises_error_for_unhashable_items(self):
        """Unhashable types (lists, dicts) correctly throw TypeError.
        This is expected behaviour for this O(n) set-based implementation."""
        bad_inputs = [
            [1, [2, 3], 1],  # nested list
            [1, {"key": "val"}, 2],  # dict items
        ]
        for bad_input in bad_inputs:
            with pytest.raises(TypeError, match="unhashable type"):
                remove_duplicates(bad_input)

    def test_raises_error_for_non_iterable_input(self):
        """Non-iterable values throw TypeError when passed"""
        invalid_inputs = [123, None, True, 3.14]
        for invalid in invalid_inputs:
            with pytest.raises(TypeError):
                remove_duplicates(invalid)
```

---
### ✅ Test Coverage Summary:
| Category | Coverage |
|---|---|
| Happy Path | ✅ Core deduplication logic, order preservation, immutable input |
| Edge Cases | ✅ Empty list, single item, all duplicates, None values, mixed types, boolean/int gotcha |
| Error Cases | ✅ Unhashable elements, non-iterable inputs |

> 💡 Important documented function properties:
> 1.  Runs in `O(n)` time complexity
> 2.  Never modifies the original input list
> 3.  Strictly preserves order of first occurrence
> 4.  Follows standard Python equality/hash rules
> 5.  Only works with lists of hashable elements
